items.title: JHEEM Portal
items.description: A public web platform presenting the Johns Hopkins HIV Epidemiologic & Economic Model (JHEEM): Ryan White funding-disruption analyses (31 metro areas; 11- and 30-state studies), CDC-testing cessation (18 states), HIV aging projections (24 states), and on-demand custom simulations. Containerized R models generate results through automated pipelines; a Next.js frontend serves them.

items.title: Reproducible modeling infrastructure
items.description: A shared Docker base image and family of model containers (renv-pinned R, with model engines version-matched to their simulation data), a release system distributing 180GB+ of simulation outputs, and a CI/CD pipeline that builds, versions, validates, and publishes the team's data assets — replacing manual file-link sharing with reproducible, traceable releases.

items.title: Computational Epidemiology Lab site + Global HIV Aging app
items.description: The Johns Hopkins Computational Epidemiology Lab website, including an interactive Global HIV Aging application that lets users explore age-distribution projections across countries.
